content_of_post_to_answer: How do you keep a bad season of life from turning into a cynical personality?
answer: Bad seasons don’t usually create cynicism; uncorrected interpretations do. When enough things go wrong in a row, the mind starts treating a narrow sample as a full model of people and life. The safeguard is not forced positivity but refusing final conclusions—keep your judgments labeled as ‘during this period’, not ‘about how things are’.
